page.content: In recent months, the dynamics of the LNG export market have been significantly influenced by various factors, including production volumes by basin and fluctuating petrochemical feedstock costs.

lines.text: In recent months, the dynamics of the LNG export market have been significantly influenced by various factors, including
production volumes by basin and fluctuating petrochemical feedstock costs.

page.content: As illustrated in the accompanying table, the throughput at LNG export terminals has shown a notable trend, with March recording a throughput of 30.0 million cubic feet per day, followed by a slight decline in April to 28.0 million cubic feet per day, and a further reduction to 27.5 million cubic feet per day in May. This decline coincides with the changing production volumes recorded across different basins, particularly Basin A, which contributed an impressive 500,000 barrels per day to overall supply. Furthermore, the associated revenue generated from these operations is noteworthy, with a reported figure of CAD 35 million, underscoring the economic implications of production fluctuations.

lines.text: As illustrated in the accompanying table, the throughput at LNG export terminals has shown a notable trend, with March

volumes recorded across different basins, particularly Basin A, which contributed an impressive 500,000 barrels per day to

page.content: Additionally, the cost of ethylene feedstock, which stood at 5.2 CAD per ton, has positioned itself as a critical determinant in shaping the profitability of petrochemical production in the region.

lines.text: Additionally, the cost of ethylene feedstock, which stood at 5.2 CAD per ton, has positioned itself as a critical determinant in
shaping the profitability of petrochemical production in the region.

page.content: Thus, understanding these metrics is essential for stakeholders navigating the evolving landscape of the North American LNG sector.
